intro: Every classification this platform shows rests on published, peer-reviewed frameworks. The background below grounds each pillar of tertiary analysis in its foundational literature (source: PubMed). This is why a community center's report can be trusted at the same bar as a university hospital's.

sections.summary: Somatic variants are tiered by clinical significance using two complementary frameworks. AMP/ASCO/CAP defines a four-tier system (Tier I strong, II potential, III unknown, IV benign) for standardized reporting; ESCAT ranks how ready a molecular target is for clinical decisions (Tier I ready for routine use → Tier X no evidence). This platform shows the ESCAT tier as its primary actionability signal.

citations.title: Standards and Guidelines for the Interpretation and Reporting of Sequence Variants in Cancer: A Joint Consensus Recommendation of AMP, ASCO, and CAP.
A framework to rank genomic alterations as targets for cancer precision medicine: the ESMO Scale for Clinical Actionability of molecular Targets (ESCAT).

sections.summary: OncoKB is a curated precision-oncology knowledge base that maps specific alterations to therapeutic implications with defined evidence levels (Level 1 FDA-recognised biomarker → Level 4 compelling biological evidence; R1/R2 for resistance). Matched therapies and their levels in this platform are OncoKB annotations.

citations.title: OncoKB: A Precision Oncology Knowledge Base.

sections.summary: High tumour mutational burden (≥10 mut/Mb) is a tissue-agnostic predictor of benefit from PD-1 blockade, established in the KEYNOTE-158 basket study and underpinning the pan-tumour pembrolizumab indication.

citations.title: Association of tumour mutational burden with outcomes in patients with advanced solid tumours treated with pembrolizumab: KEYNOTE-158.

sections.summary: Microsatellite-instability-high / mismatch-repair-deficient tumours respond durably to immune checkpoint blockade; KEYNOTE-177 established first-line pembrolizumab over chemotherapy in MSI-H metastatic colorectal cancer.

citations.title: Pembrolizumab versus chemotherapy for microsatellite instability-high or mismatch repair-deficient metastatic colorectal cancer (KEYNOTE-177): final analysis.

sections.summary: Homologous-recombination deficiency (BRCA mutation or a high genomic-instability score) predicts benefit from PARP inhibition; PAOLA-1 established olaparib plus bevacizumab maintenance in HRD-positive advanced ovarian cancer.

citations.title: Olaparib plus Bevacizumab as First-Line Maintenance in Ovarian Cancer (PAOLA-1).

sections.summary: Real-world comprehensive genomic profiling platforms report actionable findings in roughly 20–40% of advanced solid tumours — the MONDTI precision-medicine platform is a representative single-institution cohort. Our own single-center pan-cancer cohort (n=62) sits within this range (33.9% on-label match), showing a community center can reproduce academic actionability rates.

citations.title: Interim analysis of a real-world precision medicine platform for molecular profiling of metastatic or advanced cancers: MONDTI.

attribution: Literature retrieved via PubMed. Each citation links to its DOI. This platform does not replace expert review — it makes the evidence behind every recommendation one click away.
